snippet.title: PADDINGTON IN PERU – Official Trailer (HD)
snippet.description:
More adventure than any bear can bear.

Paddington gets lost in the jungle in #PaddingtonInPeru – coming exclusively to theatres February 14.

Follow Us on Social:
https://www.facebook.com/PaddingtonMovie
https://twitter.com/paddingtonmovie
https://www.instagram.com/paddingtonmovie
https://www.tiktok.com/@sonypictures

Visit our site: https://paddingtonmovie.com/

Subscribe to the Sony Pictures YouTube Channel for more exclusive content: http://bit.ly/SonyPicsSubscribe 

Full of Paddington’s signature blend of wit, charm, and laugh-out-loud humor, Paddington in Peru finds the beloved, marmalade-loving bear lost in the jungle on an exciting, high-stakes adventure. When Paddington discovers his beloved Aunt Lucy has gone missing from the Home for Retired Bears, he and the Brown family head to the wilds of Peru to look for her, the only clue to her whereabouts a spot marked on an enigmatic map. Determined to solve the mystery, Paddington embarks on a thrilling quest through the rainforests of the Amazon to find his aunt…and may also uncover one of the world’s most legendary treasures.
 
The third Paddington film stars Hugh Bonneville, Emily Mortimer, Julie Walters, Jim Broadbent, Imelda Staunton, Carla Tous, Madeleine Harris, Samuel Joslin, Olivia Colman, Antonio Banderas, and Ben Whishaw returning as the voice of Paddington.
 
Directed by:​​
Dougal Wilson​
 
Screenplay by: ​​
Mark Burton and Jon Foster & James Lamont​​
​
Story by:​​​​
Paul King
​​​​​Simon Farnaby
Mark Burton
 
“Paddington Bear” created by:​​
Michael Bond​​
 
Produced by: ​​​​
Rosie Alison​
     
Executive Producers:​​​
Anna Marsh
​​​​​Ron Halpern
​​​​​Dan MacRae
Paul King
​​​​​Jeffrey Clifford
​​​​​Rob Silva
​​​​​Tim Wellspring
 
Cast:​
Hugh Bonneville
​Emily Mortimer
​Julie Walters
​Jim Broadbent
​Imelda Staunton
​Carla Tous
​Madeleine Harris
​Samuel Joslin
​with Olivia Colman
​with Antonio Banderas
​and Ben Whishaw as the voice of Paddington
 
This film is rated PG by the Motion Picture Association for the following reasons: action, mild rude humor and some thematic elements. Credits not final
